title: Linux PrivEsc — Enumeration
excerpt: Systematic Linux privilege escalation enumeration — system info, users, network, processes, cron jobs, and automated tools (LinPEAS, LSE, pspy).

title: Linux PrivEsc — SUID/SGID Abuse
excerpt: Find and exploit SUID/SGID binaries for privilege escalation — bash, find, vim, python, perl, ruby, shared object injection, and GTFOBins reference.

title: Linux PrivEsc — Sudo Abuse
excerpt: Exploit sudo misconfigurations for Linux privilege escalation — NOPASSWD entries, GTFOBins sudo abuse, LD_PRELOAD, and wildcard injection techniques.

title: Linux PrivEsc — Linux Capabilities
excerpt: Exploit Linux capabilities (cap_setuid, cap_sys_ptrace, cap_dac_override, cap_dac_read_search) — find with getcap, exploit with python, ruby, perl, gdb, tar.

title: Linux PrivEsc — Cron Job Abuse
excerpt: Exploit Linux cron jobs for privilege escalation — writable scripts, PATH hijacking, tar/rsync wildcard injection, and pspy process monitoring.

title: Linux PrivEsc — Writable Files & PATH Hijacking
excerpt: Find world-writable files and directories, exploit PATH hijacking, write to /etc/passwd or /etc/shadow, and abuse library load order for Linux privilege escalation.

title: Linux PrivEsc — Docker / LXD / Group Abuse
excerpt: Exploit Docker, LXD, disk, adm, shadow, and video group memberships for Linux privilege escalation — full chains for container escapes and filesystem access.

title: Windows PrivEsc — Enumeration
excerpt: Systematic Windows privilege escalation enumeration — system info, network, users, services, installed software, WinPEAS/Seatbelt/PowerUp, PowerShell history, and interesting files.

title: Windows PrivEsc — Token Privilege Abuse
excerpt: Abuse Windows token privileges for privilege escalation — SeImpersonatePrivilege with GodPotato/PrintSpoofer, SeBackupPrivilege for NTDS.dit, SeDebugPrivilege for LSASS dump.

title: Windows PrivEsc — Service Misconfigurations
excerpt: Exploit Windows service misconfigurations — unquoted service paths, weak service permissions, writable binaries, DLL hijacking, AlwaysInstallElevated, and scheduled task abuse.

title: Windows PrivEsc — Credential Hunting
excerpt: Hunt Windows credentials — registry (AutoLogon, VNC, PuTTY), config files, PowerShell history, Credential Manager, SAM/SYSTEM dump via shadow copy, LSASS dump, and DPAPI decryption.
